user_query:
I'm coordinating a 'Book Launch Logistics Sync' for our team and need you to determine the best meeting time, prepare follow-ups from our last call, and draft a single invitation email. Please work entirely from the provided inputs and produce the outputs below under the outputs/ directory.

Inputs to use (all provided in input/):
- input/stakeholders.csv — stakeholders with roles, priority levels, and emails
- input/slots.csv — candidate meeting slots (ISO start/end)
- input/availability.csv — each stakeholder’s availability per slot
- input/rooms.csv — rooms with capacity and slot availability
- input/transcript.txt — raw notes from our prior planning call
- input/agenda.md — the agenda outline for the upcoming sync

What to produce:
1) outputs/slot_ranking.csv — the ranked, eligible slots with the room selection used for capacity:
   - Include columns exactly: slot_id,slot_start,slot_end,required_available_count,important_available_count,optional_available_count,attendees_available_count,score,eligible_room,room_capacity
   - A slot is eligible only if: (a) every stakeholder with priority = Required is available in that slot; and (b) at least one room is available for that slot with capacity >= the number of stakeholders available in that slot.
   - Scoring formula: score = 3*(# Required available) + 2*(# Important available) + 1*(# Optional available).
   - Choose eligible_room as the smallest-capacity room that is available in that slot and can fit the attendees_available_count; room_capacity should reflect that room’s capacity.
   - List only eligible slots, sorted by: (1) score descending, then (2) slot_start ascending.
   - Include all eligible slots in the file (top N, where N is how many qualify), to allow verification.

2) outputs/selected_slot.json — the chosen meeting slot and room as JSON with keys: slot_id, slot_start, slot_end, room_name, room_capacity, rationale. Select the top-ranked slot from outputs/slot_ranking.csv. If there is a tie on score, prefer the earlier slot_start.

3) outputs/action_items.csv — action items extracted from input/transcript.txt with columns exactly: description,owner,due_date,status. Include each action with:
   - owner matching a stakeholder name in input/stakeholders.csv (exclude any actions whose owner is not in stakeholders.csv),
   - due_date as YYYY-MM-DD (as stated in transcript),
   - status set to Pending.

4) outputs/meeting_notes.md — a concise summary derived from input/transcript.txt, with two clearly labeled sections: Decisions and Action Items. List all decisions you find. For action items, make sure they match the rows in outputs/action_items.csv (same description, owner, due_date). This consistency will be used to verify correctness.

5) outputs/invite_email.txt — a plain-text draft email to all stakeholders:
   - To line: comma-separated emails of all stakeholders from input/stakeholders.csv.
   - Subject: Propose the subject as "Book Launch Logistics Sync – [Selected Date Time]" using the selected slot.
   - Body must include: (a) the selected meeting date/time and chosen room/location; (b) up to two alternate slots from outputs/slot_ranking.csv in rank order (if fewer than two alternates are eligible, include whatever is available); (c) the agenda items from input/agenda.md as a bullet list; (d) a short bullet list of action items due on or before the selected meeting date (use outputs/action_items.csv and filter by due_date <= selected slot date); and (e) a clear call to confirm availability.

Important constraints:
- Use input/stakeholders.csv for roles and email addresses; do not invent attendees.
- Compute eligibility and scores strictly from input/slots.csv, input/availability.csv, and input/rooms.csv using the rules above.
- Rank and selection must be traceable from outputs/slot_ranking.csv to outputs/selected_slot.json and reflect the tie-breaking rule.
- Ensure the owners in outputs/action_items.csv exactly match stakeholder names in input/stakeholders.csv so cross-checking is possible.
- If no slots are eligible, still create outputs/slot_ranking.csv as an empty file with only the header row and outputs/selected_slot.json with rationale explaining why none qualified; however, with the provided inputs, at least one slot should qualify.

Deliverables must be exactly at the specified output paths and use the specified columns/keys where indicated. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
